title: (flake) RHEL 9.6 aarch64 system tests: root-local `not ok 149 |080| podman pause/unpause`
body:
### Issue Description

```
not ok 149 |080| podman pause/unpause in 12198ms
# tags: ci:parallel distro-integration
# (from function `bail-now' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 197,
#  from function `is' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 1146,
#  in test file test/system/080-pause.bats, line 51)
#   `is "$max_delta" "[3456]" "delta t between paused and restarted"' failed
#

# [23:38:36.111403493] d493c9dc59cde22d400908ed85b795e4a964bb93c4cd37aefff2afd7f80b83f3
#

# [23:38:36.239096311] 1749857916
#
# [23:38:36.248907986] # /var/ARTIFACTS/work-root-localol3ctf7i/plans/system/root-local/tree/bin/podman --noout pause d493c9dc59cde22d400908ed85b795e4a964bb93c4cd37aefff2afd7f80b83f3

# [23:38:36.424116992] # /var/ARTIFACTS/work-root-localol3ctf7i/plans/system/root-local/tree/bin/podman inspect --format {{.State.Status}} d493c9dc59cde22d400908ed85b795e4a964bb93c4cd37aefff2afd7f80b83f3
# [23:38:36.490583179] paused
#
# [23:38:39.503766038] # /var/ARTIFACTS/work-root-localol3ctf7i/plans/system/root-local/tree/bin/podman ps -a --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}} {{.Status}}

# [23:38:44.542584538] aaac03266c3c c-src-crt-t119-m54ajvtn Created
# a28e38b0c9ff jolly_morse Up 9 seconds

# b5d46ac64bec angry_morse Up 3 seconds
# d99c433fcd59 romantic_kalam Up 1 second
# 15eb5ee2c10a hardcore_bassi Up Less than a second
# e1badcf1abdd c-src-crt-t122-6pssphlh Created
# 94bf328e7852 recursing_gould Created

# [23:38:45.557522972] # /var/ARTIFACTS/work-root-localol3ctf7i/plans/system/root-local/tree/bin/podman logs d493c9dc59cde22d400908ed85b795e4a964bb93c4cd37aefff2afd7f80b83f3
```

comments.body: Are the VMs these tests are being run on exceptionally slow? The test notes that it should take 4-5 seconds to pause/unpause a container, and allows for up to 6. It's taking 8. That's twice as slow as the baseline we set for our upstream CI VMs.
Agree that timing seems super concerning
See the [VM config](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26183/files#diff-7c6a2b9d827b75e18a7febaec86c0220d24abef169eb08302e56f7bb6601f86dR45-R53) . That's applied to all VMs used in the tests.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I don't know if we want to do a full overhaul of timeouts throughout the test suite. Is it allowable to increase available memory?
